role_summary: SNCC organizer and photographer in Alabama and Mississippi from 1963; staff photographer for Black Star photo agency; co-founder (1981) of Ganados del Valle, the New Mexico rural Hispanic cooperative; MacArthur Foundation fellow, 1990.
ai_reading: Varela's top semantic neighbors are Betty Garman Robinson (entry 7, count 132), Dr. Doris Derby (entry 32), Charles McLaurin (entry 17), Martha Prescod Norman Noonan (entry 83), and John Churchville (entry 63). The cluster is the SNCC-allied-organizers-and-photographers cohort. The embedding therefore reads her as a SNCC voice rather than as a Chicana voice the cultural reading often foregrounds (Varela is one of the relatively few Latina figures in the civil rights movement record). Her Individual Conscience vs Collective Discipline position at -0.42 reads her testimony toward Individual Conscience, an unexpected pull for a photographer whose post-SNCC work organized rural Hispanic cooperatives (Ganados del Valle from 1981), which is by definition collective enterprise; the embedding may be catching the documentarian's eye in her transcript more than the organizer's hand.
biographical_paragraph: Varela was born in January 1940 in Pennsylvania and joined SNCC in 1963, working in Alabama and Mississippi as both an organizer and a photographer; her Black Star photo-agency credentials placed her work in the periodicals of the period [src: 2]. Julian Bond's 2011 University Press of Mississippi book This Light of Ours: Activist Photographers of the Civil Rights Movement documents her photographic work alongside that of her SNCC colleagues [src: 2]. In 1981 she co-founded Ganados del Valle, a wool-and-sheep cooperative in northern New Mexico that has continued the rural Hispanic land-grant tradition through her organizing; in 1990 she received a MacArthur Foundation fellowship in recognition of that work [src: 3]. Her Library of Congress oral history is entry 81 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1].

sources.title: Maria Varela oral history
